claudeMd:
# Team project

Be careful.
Try to write good code.
Always handle errors properly and make sure things work.
Use the right tool for the job.

probe..claude/skills/audit/SKILL.md:
---
name: audit
description: >-
  Grades every rule in the project's CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/ for structural
  clarity — verb strength, framing, trigger distance, loading scope, position in
  the file, concreteness — and detects rules that would work better as hooks,
  skills, or subagents. Also grades each project skill's frontmatter description
  against the trigger recipe. Do NOT use to review code, PRs, or non-Claude config
  like eslint.
when_to_use: >-
  Trigger when the user wants feedback on existing rule files: "are my rules any
  good", "check my CLAUDE.md", "grade my instruction files", "which rules are
  weak or vague", "audit my rules", or "which rules should be hooks".
---

# audit

When this skill is invoked, do these steps in order:

1. Read the project's `CLAUDE.md`.
2. Write `audit-report.md` grading each rule you find. Write one line per rule in the form `<short rule label>: grade <A-F>`, then a final line `corpus grade: <A-F>`.
3. Tell the user the grades are in `audit-report.md`.

task.prompt: Are the rules in my CLAUDE.md any good? Grade my instruction file and tell me which ones are weak.
